# seca Provider

The **seca** provider manages resources on the SECA (Sovereign European Cloud API) platform.

Configure the provider with a bearer token, tenant ID, and region. Use `global_providers` to point at the SECA control-plane endpoints for your region. The optional `retry` block fine-tunes polling behaviour for async operations.

## Example Usage

```terraform
provider "seca" {
  token  = "test-token"
  tenant = "tenant-1"
  region = "region-1"
  retry = {
    delay        = 30 # seconds
    interval     = 10 # seconds
    max_attempts = 5
  }
  global_providers = {
    region_v1        = "http://localhost:3000/providers/seca.region",
    authorization_v1 = "http://localhost:3000/providers/seca.authorization"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `global_providers` (Attributes) Control-plane endpoint URLs. Obtain these from your SECA account portal. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--global_providers))
- `region` (String) Region identifier (e.g. `eu-central-1`). Determines which regional endpoints are used.
- `tenant` (String) Tenant (organisation) identifier. All resources are created under this tenant.
- `token` (String) Bearer token used to authenticate API requests. Set via `SECA_TOKEN` environment variable.

### Optional

- `retry` (Attributes) Fine-grained polling configuration for async operations. All fields are optional; omitted fields inherit provider defaults. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--retry))

<a id="nestedatt--global_providers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `global_providers`

Required:

- `region_v1` (String) URL of the RegionV1 provider endpoint (e.g. `https://api.seca.cloud/providers/seca.region`).

Optional:

- `authorization_v1` (String) URL of the AuthorizationV1 provider endpoint. Required when managing IAM roles and role assignments.


<a id="nestedatt--retry"></a>
### Nested Schema for `retry`

Optional:

- `delay` (Number) Initial wait in seconds before the first status poll. Default: 30.
- `interval` (Number) Wait in seconds between subsequent status polls. Default: 10.
- `max_attempts` (Number) Maximum number of polling attempts before failing. Default: 5. Superseded by per-resource `timeouts` blocks.
